{\n298\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n299\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n300\t\t\topt(config)\n301\t\t}\n302\t\n303\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n304\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n305\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n306\t\n307\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n308\t\t\tformatter = =============================================================================\n402\t\n403\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\n404\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n405\t\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n406\t\tif err != nil {\n407\t\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n408\t\t}\n409\t\n410\t\tdesc, err = nil\n417\t}\n418\t\n419\t// =============================================================================\n420\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n421\t// =============================================================================\n422\t\n423\t// FormatError creates a basic formatted error message string.\n424\t// This is a simplified formatting function for basic error message display.\n425\t// Unlike the ValidationFormatter which returns ValidationError structs,\n426\t// this function returns a simple formatted string for quick error display.\n427\t//\n428\t// This function supports both string-based error types (for backward compatibility)\n429\t// and ErrorType enum for type-safe error classification. The ErrorType enum\n430\t// provides consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n431\t//\n432\t// Parameters:\n433\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\")\n434\t//   - message: The error message content\n435\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n436\t//\n437\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n438\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n439\t//\n440\t// Example usage:\n441\t//\n442\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"status_code\", \"Expected 200 but got 404\", \"response\")\n443\t//\t// Returns: \"[status_code] response: Expected 200 but got 404\"\n444\t//\n445\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"\")\n446\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] This field is required\"\n447\t//\n448\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"\", \"Something went wrong\", \"\")\n449\t//\t// Returns: \"[error] Something went wrong\"\n450\t//\n451\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"validation\", \"\", \"email\")\n452\t//\t// Returns: \"[validation] email: email validation failed\"\n453\tfunc FormatError(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n454\t\t// Convert string errorType to ErrorType enum for consistent classification\n455\t\t_ = ErrorTypeFromString(errorType) // Verify error type is valid\n456\t\n457\t\t// Extract field name from variadic args\n458\t\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n459\t\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n460\t\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n461\t\t}\n462\t\n463\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n464\t\tif message == \"\" {\n465\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n466\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n467\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n468\t\t\t} else {\n469\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n470\t\t\t}\n471\t\t}\n472\t\n473\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage which is already integrated with ErrorType\n474\t\t// This ensures consistent error classification across all errors\n475\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n476\t}\n477\t\n478\t// FormatErrorWithType creates a basic formatted error message string using ErrorType enum.\n479\t// This function provides type-safe error classification using the ErrorType enum\n480\t// instead of string-based error types.\n481\t//\n482\t// This is the preferred method for error formatting when you have an ErrorType enum\n483\t// value, as it ensures consistent error classification and prevents typos.\n484\t//\n485\t// Parameters:\n486\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n487\t//   - message: The error message content\n488\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n489\t//\n490\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n491\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n492\t//\n493\t// Example usage:\n494\t//\n495\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n496\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n497\t//\n498\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n499\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n500\t//\n501\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n502\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n503\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n504\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n505\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n506\t\n507\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n508\t\tif message == \"\" {\n509\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n510\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n511\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n512\t\t\t} else {\n513\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n514\t\t\t}\n515\t\t}\n516\t\n517\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n518\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n519\t}\n520\t\n521\t// =============================================================================\n522\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n523\t// =============================================================================\n524\t\n525\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n526\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n527\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n528\t//\n529\t// Parameters:\n530\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n531\t//\n532\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n533\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n534\t//\n535\t// Example usage:\n536\t//\n537\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n538\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n539\t//\n540\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n541\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n542\t//\n543\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n544\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n545\t//\n546\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n547\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n548\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n549\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n550\t\n551\t\t// Handle empty path\n552\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n553\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n554\t\t}\n555\t\n556\t\t// Parse and normalize the field {\n\t\topt(config)\n\t}\n\n\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n\t\tWithActual(actual)\n\n\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n\t}\n\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n\t}\n\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n\t}\n\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n\t}\n\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n\t}\n\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n\t}\n\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n\t}\n\n\treturn {\n\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n\t\tdesc = \"\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatError creates a basic formatted error message string.\n// This is a simplified formatting function for basic error message display.\n// Unlike the ValidationFormatter which returns ValidationError structs,\n// this function returns a simple formatted string for quick error display.\n//\n// This function supports both string-based error types (for backward compatibility)\n// and ErrorType enum for type-safe error classification. The ErrorType enum\n// provides consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The type/category of error (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\")\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"status_code\", \"Expected 200 but got 404\", \"response\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[status_code] response: Expected 200 but got 404\"\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] This field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"\", \"Something went wrong\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[error] Something went wrong\"\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"validation\", \"\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[validation] email: email validation failed\"\nfunc FormatError(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n\t// Convert string errorType to ErrorType enum for consistent classification\n\t_ = ErrorTypeFromString(errorType) // Verify error type is valid\n\n\t// Extract field name from variadic args\n\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n\tif message == \"\" {\n\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Use FormatErrorMessage which is already integrated with ErrorType\n\t// This ensures consistent error classification across all errors\n\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithType creates a basic formatted error message string using ErrorType enum.\n// This function provides type-safe error classification using the ErrorType enum\n// instead of string-based error types.\n//\n// This is the preferred method for error formatting when you have an ErrorType enum\n// value, as it ensures consistent error classification and prevents typos.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n\n\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n\tif message == \"\" {\n\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\nfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n\n\t// Handle empty path\n\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n}\n\n// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\nfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n\tif path == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n\tvar result []string\n\n\tfor _, part := range parts {\n\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n\t\tif part == \"\" {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Regular field name\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif len(result) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n}\n\n// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\nfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n\tif s == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n\tif s[0] == '-' {\n\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t\ts = s[1:]\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if all characters are digits\n\tfor _, r := range s {\n\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\nfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n}\n\n","numLines":632,"startLine":1,"totalLines":632}}}

\"data.users[0].email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"multiple array indices\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"data.0.items.1.name\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"response\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"response.data[0].items[1].name\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"parent with array index\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"users.0\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users[0].email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"both parent and field with array indices\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"profile.email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"users.0\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users[0].profile.email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"array index at start of field\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"0.email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"users\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users.0.email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"empty field name with array parent\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"users.0\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users[0]\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"deeply nested with array indices\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"items.0.data.values.1.field\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"response\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"response.items[0].data.values[1].field\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"negative array index\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"users.-1.email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"response\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"response.users[-1].email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"large array index\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"users.12345.name\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users[12345].name\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatFieldRef(tt.fieldName, tt.parent)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatFieldRef() = %v, want %v\", result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatFieldLocationWith(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname        string\n\t\tfieldName   string\n\t\tpattern     string\n\t\tactualValue interface{}\n\t\texpected    string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    ValidationError\n\t\tincludeSeverity bool\n\t\texpected        string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"with all fields and severity\",\n\t\t\terr: ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\tErrorType: 5\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tincludeSeverity: true,\n\t\t\texpected:        \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required (expected: value) [line 5]\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"with
